title: Contact360 public and private apis and endpoints

sections.title: REST vs GraphQL vs gRPC decision (REST primary, GraphQL internal)
API versioning strategy (URL path v1/v2, header-based, deprecation)
API style guide (URL patterns, naming conventions, response format)
Request body schemas (JSON, field validation, required vs optional)
Response body standards (data envelope, metadata, pagination, errors)
Error response format (error codes, messages, field-level errors)
Input validation standards (type checking, length limits, business rules)
Rate limit error responses (429 status, retry-after headers)
API key authentication (header, query param, rotation)
OAuth2 authentication (authorization code flow, client credentials)
JWT tokens (access token, refresh token, expiry, signing)
Role-based access control (RBAC) on endpoints (user, manager, admin)
Organization-level isolation (org_id check on all endpoints)
Session creation & validation (login flow, token issuance)
Filter operators (eq, neq, gt, lt, in, contains, startswith)
Default sort order (by ID, by created_at, by relevance)
Webhook event payload format (timestamp, org_id, event_type, data)
Webhook event types catalog (contact.created, campaign.sent, etc.)
Webhook retry policy (exponential backoff, max retries)
Webhook rate limiting (max deliveries per hour)
Job submission pattern (POST returns job_id, GET polls status)
Job status endpoint (pending, running, completed, failed)
Overage handling (burst limit, disable features, alert)
Burst limit (allow temporary spike over rate limit)
Concurrent request limits (max N parallel requests)
Connection limits (max N open connections per org)
Version naming (v1, v2, URL path vs header)
Multi-version support (run v1 and v2 simultaneously)
API guide & tutorials (getting started, common patterns)
Python SDK (auto-generated, tested, published to PyPI)
API code samples (cURL, Python, JavaScript, Go)
Common workflows (create contact + enrich + send email)
Error handling examples (retry logic, exponential backoff)
Breaking change detection (test suite catches breaking changes)
Backward compatibility tests (old clients still work)
Load test scenarios (100 concurrent users, spike to 1000)
PII masking in logs & responses (don't expose raw data)
OWASP Top 10 (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF protection)
Audit logging (all API calls logged, searchable)
Runbooks for API incidents (high latency, errors, degradation)
Sandbox environment (test API without hitting prod)
Webhook pattern (server pushes events to client)
API support channels (Discord, GitHub discussions, email)
Community examples & integrations (blog posts, GitHub repos)
Changelog format (date, version, changes by category)
Release checklist (docs updated, tests pass, changelog)
